_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in using multiple analytic tools, databases, and techniques (e.g., Analyst’s Notebook, A-Space, Anchory, M3, divergent/convergent thinking, link charts, matrices, etc.).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and executing complex analytical projects independently, including scoping, methodology selection, execution, and executive-level communication of findings.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.578), AB-S-007 (AB, cosine: 0.568).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.492). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [analyst, analytic, anchory, charts, convergent, databases] vs WIDAI-only terms [analytical, communication, complex, designing, executing, execution] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.845 (P=0.830, R=0.859). R exceeds P by 0.029 — the WIDAI KSA's 19 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 21 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Skill in using multiple analytic tools, databases, and techniques (e.g., Analyst" and "Skill in designing and executing complex analytical projects independently, incl" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.605 > STS 0.492 (gap +0.114). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.3%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [analyst, analytic, anchory, charts, convergent, databases]. WIDAI-only: [analytical, communication, complex, designing, executing, execution].
